demoNotice: All organizations, people, terms, and events in these packets are fictional and synthetic.

packets.title: Northstar renewal review
packets.summary: Facilities support agreement, 2026 amendment, and internal handoff note.
packets.suggestedQuestion: What requires approval and what is the response-time obligation?

clauses.text: Synthetic-data notice: this fictional agreement exists only for a portfolio demonstration.
Vendor: Northstar Facilities Group provides preventive and corrective maintenance for the fictional Redwood office network.
Term: July 1, 2025 through June 30, 2026, with one optional twelve-month renewal.
Annual fee: $128,400, invoiced in equal monthly installments.
High-severity response: a qualified technician must acknowledge the incident within four business hours.
Approval threshold: change orders above $5,000 require written approval from the procurement operations manager.
Non-renewal notice: either party must provide notice at least 45 calendar days before the term ends.
Data scope: service records may contain equipment identifiers and business contact roles but no payment-card or patient data.
Synthetic-data notice: this fictional amendment exists only for a portfolio demonstration.
Vendor: Northstar Facilities Group provides preventive and corrective maintenance for the fictional Redwood office network.
Term: July 1, 2026 through June 30, 2027, with one optional twelve-month renewal.
Annual fee: $136,800, invoiced in equal monthly installments.
High-severity response: a qualified technician must acknowledge the incident within two business hours.
Approval threshold: change orders above $2,500 require written approval from the procurement operations manager.
Non-renewal notice: either party must provide notice at least 60 calendar days before the term ends.
Data scope: service records may contain equipment identifiers and business contact roles but no payment-card or patient data.
Synthetic-data notice: this fictional handoff contains no customer or employee records.
Operations requested the shorter two-hour response target after three fictional after-hours equipment failures.
Finance has not approved the annual fee increase; the reviewer must record that decision before renewal.
The lower change-order threshold creates more approval events and should be confirmed with the procurement operations manager.
No external message or renewal action may be sent from this demonstration.

packets.title: Harborline warehouse support
packets.summary: Warehouse device support agreement and proposed peak-season addendum.
packets.suggestedQuestion: Which service levels changed and when can the agreement be ended?

clauses.text: Synthetic-data notice: all warehouse sites and commercial terms are fictional.
Vendor: Harborline Systems supports handheld scanners at three fictional distribution centers.
Term: October 1, 2025 through September 30, 2026, without automatic renewal.
Annual fee: $84,000, excluding replacement hardware.
High-severity response: remote support must respond within 90 minutes during staffed warehouse hours.
Approval threshold: hardware purchases above $1,500 require warehouse operations director approval.
Non-renewal notice: no notice is required because the schedule ends on its stated date.
Data scope: support logs include device identifiers, shift codes, and fictional site names.
Synthetic-data notice: all warehouse sites and commercial terms are fictional.
Vendor: Harborline Systems supports handheld scanners at three fictional distribution centers.
Term: October 1, 2026 through January 31, 2027, without automatic renewal.
Annual fee: $42,000 for the four-month peak-season period, excluding replacement hardware.
High-severity response: remote support must respond within 30 minutes at any time during the peak-season term.
Approval threshold: hardware purchases above $1,500 require warehouse operations director approval.
Non-renewal notice: either party may end the addendum with 14 calendar days written notice.
Data scope: support logs include device identifiers, shift codes, and fictional site names.
Synthetic-data notice: this fictional note contains no worker names or real locations.
The proposed coverage becomes continuous, rather than limited to staffed warehouse hours.
The reviewer should confirm whether the 14-day termination right is operationally acceptable during peak volume.
No demand forecast is included in this packet, so the support level should not be treated as financially justified by the demo.
